title: Bowerbird Schema
description: Schema for defining types, fields, and configuration for markdown vaults

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON Schema for editor support

schemaVersion.description: User-controlled schema content version for migrations (semver)

traits.description: Reusable field bundles composed into types via each type's `traits` array. Composition (`also-has`) alongside `extends` inheritance (`is-a`). Optional; schemas without traits are unchanged.

trait.description: A reusable bundle of fields composed into a type. Traits are flat: they carry only fields (and an optional description, plus an optional recurrence block) and cannot extend types or compose other traits.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this trait bundles and when to use it. Surfaced by `bwrb schema list`.

fields.description: Field definitions contributed by this trait

recurrence.description: Event-driven, spawn-on-transition recurrence (#107). When a field transitions into a value (e.g. status enters done), spawn a successor note from a template, offsetting the successor's date field from a predecessor date field. No cron, no daemon, no LLM.

on.description: Trigger transition, written as `<field> = <value>` (e.g. "status = done"). The successor is spawned when the trigger field transitions INTO this value.

template.description: Template to spawn the successor from. Defaults to the completed note's type default template (a task begets a task). A named template can spawn a different type (finish "draft" -> spawn "review").

name_template.description: Optional name template for the successor (#679). Interpolated with the same tokens as filename patterns -- {name} (the predecessor's name), {date} / {date:FORMAT} (today), and any predecessor field {field} -- then sanitized for a filename (e.g. "Review: {name}" -> "Review Chapter One"). Gives a cross-type successor a meaningful, distinct name instead of a numeric suffix. When omitted, the predecessor's name is carried forward. Vault-global basename uniqueness is still enforced on the result -- if the interpolated name also collides, a numeric suffix is appended on top.

set.description: Field-offset assignments for the successor. Each value is a field-offset expression `<dateField> <+|-> <duration>` (e.g. "deadline + 7d"). The base must be a date field on the predecessor. Transition-time offsets and calendar-anchored bases are not supported.

link_format.description: Link format for relation fields: wikilink ("[[Note]]") or markdown ("[Note](Note.md)")

obsidian_vault.description: Obsidian vault name for URI scheme (auto-detected if not set)

default_dashboard.description: Dashboard to run when `bwrb dashboard` is called without arguments

excluded_directories.description: Vault-root-relative directory prefixes excluded from all discovery/targeting operations (e.g., "Archive", "Templates")

date_format.description: Date format for date fields (YYYY, MM, DD tokens), e.g. YYYY-MM-DD (default), MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY

date_granularity.description: Default coarsest date precision allowed for all date fields (day = full YYYY-MM-DD, month = YYYY-MM or finer, year = YYYY or finer). Per-field `granularity` overrides this default.

allowed_extra_fields.description: Extra frontmatter fields that are allowed without warning

typeNode.description: Type definition with inheritance support. Flat v2 model: `extends` for is-a inheritance, `traits` for also-has composition, `fields` (not `frontmatter`) for field definitions. All properties are optional.

extends.description: Parent type name (implicit 'meta' if not specified). Single-parent is-a inheritance.

traits.description: Trait names composed into this type (composition alongside `extends`). Precedence: own fields > traits > inherited; later traits in the array win over earlier ones.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this type is for and when to use it. Surfaced by `bwrb schema list`.

fields.description: Field definitions, merged with ancestors and traits at load time

field_order.description: Explicit field ordering (optional; defaults to definition order)

body_sections.description: Body section definitions generated after frontmatter

recursive.description: Whether this type can contain instances of itself

output_dir.description: Directory path relative to vault root where files of this type are created (computed from hierarchy if not specified)

plural.description: Custom plural form for folder naming (e.g., 'research' instead of 'researches'). Auto-pluralized if not specified.

value.description: Static value (use $NOW for current datetime, $TODAY for date)

prompt.description: Type of prompt: text (free text), select (from enum), relation (from vault query), list (comma-separated list), date (date picker), boolean (yes/no), number (numeric input)

granularity.description: Coarsest date precision allowed for this `date` field, finer is always allowed (day = full YYYY-MM-DD, month = YYYY-MM or finer, year = YYYY or finer). Overrides the global config.date_granularity for this field.

label.description: Label shown to user for input prompts (the imperative prompt text, e.g. "Select status"). Distinct from `description`, which explains what the field is for.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this field is for and when to use it. Surfaced by `bwrb schema list`; distinct from `label`.

value.description: The option value stored in frontmatter

description.description: What this option means / when to choose it

options.description: Allowed values for select fields (inline options). Each entry is a bare value or a { value, description } pair that documents what the option means.

source.description: Type name(s) for relation prompts. When a relation's value is ambiguous because two notes share a name, path-qualify the link (e.g. `[[contexts/Betson]]`); see the search command docs for the shortest-unambiguous-form rule.

filter.description: Filter conditions for type-based source queries

multiple.description: Whether this field can hold multiple values

owned.description: Whether children referenced by this field are owned (colocate with parent)

alias.description: Field role: marks this field as holding the entity's aliases (alternate names). bwrb consults aliases during name resolution and linking, so an entity is findable by its aliases wherever it is findable by its name. The value must be an array of non-empty, unique strings (Obsidian `aliases` format).

default.description: Default value if user skips the prompt

required.description: Whether this field is required (user cannot skip)

list_format.description: For list fields, how to format the list

content_type.description: Type of content placeholder to add

prompt.description: If set, prompts user for initial content during creation

not_equals.description: Field must not equal this value

in.description: Field must be one of these values

not_in.description: Field must not be one of these values
